description_long: This object is a vintage-style biplane with a wooden appearance, featuring two stacked straight wings supported by struts. The fuselage is slender and tapers toward the tail, which has a conventional horizontal stabilizer and a single vertical fin. The front of the airplane is equipped with a large, multi-blade propeller and a fixed landing gear with two main wheels and a small tail wheel. The cockpit is open and situated just behind the upper wing, with a cutout in the fuselage for pilot access. The surface texture is matte and slightly rough, with visible wood grain patterns throughout. The overall design is reminiscent of early 20th-century aircraft, likely intended as a decorative or model piece.
description: A wooden biplane with stacked wings, open cockpit, and fixed landing gear, featuring a large front propeller and classic vintage design.
description_view_0: Front view shows the large multi-blade propeller, stacked biplane wings, and fixed landing gear.
description_view_1: Angled side view highlights the open cockpit, strut-supported wings, and the tail's horizontal and vertical stabilizers.
description_view_2: Rear view displays the tail assembly, including the single vertical fin and horizontal stabilizer, as well as the alignment of the wings.
description_view_3: Opposite angled side view reveals the propeller, open cockpit, and the full length of the fuselage and tail wheel.
attribute_wingspan_configuration: Biplane configuration with two straight, parallel wings stacked vertically and supported by interplane struts; no winglets or pronounced dihedral/anhedral angles.
attribute_engine_type: Single front-mounted piston engine with a multi-blade propeller.
attribute_tail_design: Conventional tail with a single vertical stabilizer and horizontal stabilizer.
attribute_landing_gear_type: Fixed taildragger landing gear with two main wheels and a single tail wheel.
attribute_cockpit_placement: Open, single-seat cockpit located just behind the upper wing, mid-fuselage.
